# Overview {.unnumbered}

This website looks at the **Cambridge Central School District** (Cambridge
CSD, Washington County, New York) alongside nearby districts, using public
data from the New York State Education Department (NYSED) and selected other sources. It is one of several
analyses in the [`ccs`](https://github.com/donboyd5/ccs) project; the aim is to
put Cambridge's numbers in context rather than to view them in isolation.

## Comparison groups

Different questions call for different peer sets, so the book uses two groups
that can be adjusted as the work develops:

- **Graph group (5 districts)** — used for charts, where a short list keeps
  lines legible: **Cambridge, Greenwich, Salem** (all Washington County),
  **Schuylerville** (Saratoga County), and **Hoosick Falls** (Rensselaer
  County). These are nearby small districts.
- **Table group (12 districts)** — used for tables, which can hold more rows:
  **every Washington County K-12 district** plus the two graph-group districts
  that sit just outside the county (Schuylerville and Hoosick Falls).

The table group is the broader set; the graph group is a readable subset of it.
**Putnam CSD** is left out of the "Washington County K-12" set because it is a
K-8 district -- it has no high school of its own and pays to send its high-school students elsewhere, so it is not comparable on K-12 measures.

| Group | Districts |
|---|---|
| **Graph** (charts) | Cambridge, Greenwich, Hoosick Falls, Salem, Schuylerville |
| **Table** (tables) | The graph group **+** Argyle, Fort Ann, Fort Edward, Granville, Hartford, Hudson Falls, Whitehall |

## Data and a few cautions

The underlying numbers come from NYSED's public downloads and other reputable sources; each chapter and the
[Sources](sources.qmd) appendix give the specifics. A few things to keep in mind
throughout:

- **Year labels are school-year-ending years.** "2025" means the **2024–25**
  school year. Enrollment is the BEDS-day count (first Wednesday of October).
- **Teacher counts begin in 2017–18.** So time series that depend on teachers
  (such as students per teacher) start at school-year-ending **2018**, even
  though enrollment runs back to 2015–16.
- **Students per teacher is an approximation, not official class size.** It
  divides K-12 enrollment by a district-wide teacher *headcount* (all grades,
  not full-time-equivalents). It is useful for comparison across districts and
  over time, but it is not NYSED's official pupil-teacher or average-class-size
  figure. See the [Sources](sources.qmd) appendix for detail.
- **Charter schools are excluded** — they have no district-total row in the
  source data — so these are district-operated figures.
